commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=5f52120564dfed09b33bc4a6e1f1b019f39adcea)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes strict-agentic planning-only retry detection and tests so sleep-only bash tool-use turns can retry while ordinary or shell-grouped commands remain non-retryable.

PR surface: Source +36, Tests +55. Total +91 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main rejects the reported `toolUse` sleep-only path before planning-only text detection, and the PR's own helper test demonstrates the intended decision when raw metadata is supplied.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed because the code and proof must be updated against the production metadata path; automation cannot supply the contributor's real behavior proof for this external PR.

target.title: fix(agents): detect dummy sleep planning-only turns
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the strict-agentic planning-only guard treated a `toolUse` terminal turn and any non-`update_plan` tool meta as concrete progress, so a dummy `bash` sleep could bypass the retry.
- Solution: classify `update_plan` plus a single pure `sleep` `bash`/`exec` meta as no-progress tool activity, while keeping shell groups and ordinary commands as progress.
- What changed: `resolvePlanningOnlyRetryInstruction()` now distinguishes no-progress sleep metas from real command activity and keeps ordinary or shell-grouped `bash` tool-use turns blocked from planning-only retry.
- What did NOT change: ordinary command execution, file mutations, messaging deliveries, async started tools, shell groups, and multi-tool progress still suppress planning-only retries.

Fixes #64883.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: A strict-agentic assistant turn that only says it will act and calls `bash` with `sleep 0.1` should still be treated as planning-only; `sleep 0.1; (touch /tmp/openclaw-sentinel)` and ordinary `bash` commands should not be replayed as planning-only.

Real environment tested: Local Linux checkout with the patched production `resolvePlanningOnlyRetryInstruction()` loaded through `tsx`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 exec tsx -e 'import { resolvePlanningOnlyRetryInstruction } from "./src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts"; const base = { provider: "openai", modelId: "gpt-5.4", executionContract: "strict-agentic", prompt: "Please inspect the code, make the change, and run the checks.", aborted: false, timedOut: false }; const attempt = (meta: string) => ({ assistantTexts: ["I\x27ll update the code now."], clientToolCalls: undefined, yieldDetected: false, didSendDeterministicApprovalPrompt: false, didSendViaMessagingTool: false, messagingToolSentTexts: [], messagingToolSentMediaUrls: [], messagingToolSentTargets: [], lastToolError: undefined, lastAssistant: { stopReason: "toolUse" }, itemLifecycle: { startedCount: 1, completedCount: 1, activeCount: 0 }, replayMetadata: { hadPotentialSideEffects: true, replaySafe: false }, toolMetas: [{ toolName: "bash", meta }] }); console.log("dummy sleep retry:", resolvePlanningOnlyRetryInstruction({ ...base, attempt: attempt("sleep 0.1") })?.includes("Act now") ?? false); console.log("sleep shell group retry:", resolvePlanningOnlyRetryInstruction({ ...base, attempt: attempt("sleep 0.1; (touch /tmp/openclaw-sentinel)") }) ?? "null"); console.log("ordinary bash retry:", resolvePlanningOnlyRetryInstruction({ ...base, attempt: attempt("git status --short") }) ?? "null");'
```

Evidence after fix: Console output from the patched production helper:
```text
dummy sleep retry: true
sleep shell group retry: null
ordinary bash retry: null
```

Observed result after fix: The same strict-agentic plan-only prose plus `sleep 0.1` now returns the act-now retry instruction, while `sleep 0.1; (touch /tmp/openclaw-sentinel)` and `git status --short` return `null` and are not replayed as planning-only.

What was not tested: No full embedded runner retry-exhaustion integration was added; this change is covered at the production helper seam recommended for runner decision logic.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Focused helper regression tests.
- Target test file: `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: strict-agentic `toolUse` turn with plan-only prose and `bash` meta `sleep 0.1` must return the planning-only retry instruction.
- Boundary guardrail: ordinary non-sleep `bash` meta and `sleep 0.1; (touch /tmp/openclaw-sentinel)` remain non-retryable so real command activity is still treated as progress/side effect.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: `resolvePlanningOnlyRetryInstruction()` is the production decision point that the runner calls before retrying or surfacing the strict-agentic blocked state.

## Verification

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 tsgo:core
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 tsgo:core:test
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 exec oxlint --deny=warn --ignore-path=.oxlintignore src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Verification output captured locally:

```text
Focused Vitest:
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  115 passed (115)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 25.39s

tsgo:core passed
tsgo:core:test passed
oxlint touched files passed
git diff --check passed
```

## Root Cause

- Root cause: planning-only detection counted every non-`update_plan` tool meta as progress and rejected any terminal stop reason other than `stop`, so a dummy sleep tool call with `stopReason: "toolUse"` skipped the text-based planning-only detector.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no no-progress tool classification for dummy delay commands before applying tool progress, replay-safety, item lifecycle, and `toolUse` terminal checks; the sleep classifier must stay limited to a single sleep command so shell grouping remains protected as progress.
